title: Gilia nuda
taxonomy: Gilia nuda is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae, which is a family of annual or perennial plants commonly known as the 'gloxinia' or 'Jacob's-ladder' family. This classification further places Gilia nuda within the order Ericales, a large order of flowering plants that includes many other families of flowering plants. The family Polemoniaceae is native to the Americas, and within this family, Gilia nuda can be specifically classified as a member of the genus Gilia. The genus Gilia is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as 'gilia' or 'bluebells', and they are characterized by their bell-shaped flowers. Gilia nuda is specifically classified as a member of the section Leptogyne within the genus Gilia.

intro: Gilia nuda, also known as the naked gilia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The health benefits of Gilia nuda include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress.
